name: Fudge Crostata with Raspberry Sauce
recipeIngredient: 15 oz Pillsbury All Ready Pie Crusts
recipeInstructions:
------------
Prepare pie crust according to package directions for a two-crust pie using a 10-inch tart pan with removable bottom or a 9-inch pie pan. Place 1 prepared crust in the pan and press into the bottom and up the sides. Trim edges if necessary.
Heat oven to 425°F (220°C).
In a small saucepan over low heat, melt chocolate chips with 2 tablespoons butter and 2/3 cup sugar until light and fluffy. Add almonds, 1 egg, egg yolk, and melted chocolate; blend well. Spread mixture evenly over the bottom of the pie crust-lined pan.
To make lattice top, cut the remaining crust into 1/2-inch strips. Arrange strips in a lattice design over the chocolate mixture. Trim edges and seal. In a small bowl, beat egg white until foamy and gently brush over the lattice. Bake at 425°F (220°C) for 10 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 350°F (175°C). Bake an additional 30 to 35 minutes or until crust is golden brown. Cover the edge of the pie crust with strips of foil after 15 to 20 minutes of baking to avoid excessive browning. Cool; remove sides of pan.
In a blender container or food processor bowl with metal blade, blend raspberries at high speed until smooth. Press through a fine-mesh strainer to remove seeds; discard seeds. In a small saucepan, combine raspberry puree, 3/4 cup sugar, and lemon juice; blend well. Bring mixture to a boil over medium-low heat. Boil for 3 minutes, stirring constantly. Cool.
Garnish pie with whipped cream, chocolate curls, and whole raspberries. Serve with raspberry sauce. Store in the refrigerator. Makes 10 to 12 servings.
------------
